input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a specific plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

## 1. Install Required Workloads

```bash
dotnet workload install maui-android
```

## 2. Find Correct Android SDK/NDK Versions

Check what your installed workload expects:

```bash
# Shows the required Android API level and build-tools version
dotnet workload list
grep -r "android" ~/.dotnet/sdk-manifests/ | grep -i "api\|version"
```

Or inspect your `.csproj`:
```bash
grep -i "TargetFramework\|SupportedOSPlatformVersion" YourApp.csproj
# e.g., net8.0-android → API 34
```

## 3. Install Java (JDK 17)

```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

## 4. Install Android SDK (headless, no GUI)

```bash
# Download command-line tools
mkdir -p ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
cd ~/android-sdk/cmdline-tools
curl -o tools.zip https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-latest.zip
unzip tools.zip && mv cmdline-tools latest

# Accept licenses and install required components
export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin

yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install matching platform & build-tools (adjust versions to match your TFM)
sdkmanager "platform-tools" \
           "platforms;android-34" \
           "build-tools;34.0.0"
```

## 5. Point .NET at Your SDK

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME=~/android-sdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

Or set MSBuild properties in `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <AndroidSdkDirectory>$(HOME)/android-sdk</AndroidSdkDirectory>
  <JavaSdkDirectory>/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64</JavaSdkDirectory>
</PropertyGroup>
```

## 6. Build

```bash
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -c Release
# or publish a signed APK/AAB:
dotnet publish -f net8.0-android -c Release
```

## Key Version-Matching Tips

| Your TFM | Default API | Min JDK |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| net8.0-android | 34 | 17 |
| net9.0-android | 35 | 17 |

To confirm exactly what's expected:
```bash
dotnet new android -o /tmp/check && grep "TargetFramework" /tmp/check/*.csproj
```

The workload installer (`dotnet workload install maui-android`) pulls matching Android bindings, but you still need the native SDK/build-tools at the matching API level installed via `sdkmanager`.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan to set up a headless Linux CI environment for building a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK installed but need everything else. Let me provide a concise but comprehensive plan.
